conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Send an email to a software developer asking for a product demo.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding technical jargon while maintaining a formal tone.
new_constraint: "Hey there! Just wanted to drop you a quick note about checking out your awesome product!"
instruction: Send an email to a software developer asking for a product demo. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding technical jargon while maintaining a formal tone. "Hey there! Just wanted to drop you a quick note about checking out your awesome product!"
response:
Subject: Request for a Product Demonstration

Dear [Developer's Name],

I hope this message finds you well.

I have been following the development of [Product Name] and have been impressed by the innovative approach you have taken to solve [specific problem or industry challenge]. Your focus on [mention a specific feature or value proposition, e.g., efficiency, user experience, or scalability] aligns closely with the goals of our organization.

I am writing to respectfully request a brief product demonstration. We are currently evaluating solutions to [briefly state your business need], and we believe that a live walkthrough of [Product Name] would provide valuable insights into how it could support our operations.

Could you please let me know your availability for a 15–20 minute call next week? Alternatively, if a recorded demo or a self-guided tour would be more convenient for your schedule, please let me know how we can proceed.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to the possibility of learning more about your solution.

Best regards,

[Your Full Name]  
[Your Job Title]  
[Your Company Name]  
[Your Phone Number]  
[Your Email Address]
